question: May a contracting authority negotiate the content of the procurement documents with economic operators during the course of the procurement procedure?
topic: Public procurement — binding nature of the procurement documents — contracting authority must comply with its own criteria
gold_answer: No. The procurement documents are binding on both the contracting authority and the participating operators. A contracting authority must comply strictly with the criteria it has itself established (Pippo Pizzo, C-27/15, paras 39, 42), and the procurement documents cannot be automatically supplemented with criteria not set out in them (NV Construct, C-403/21, paras 68-69). Neither party may negotiate the content of the procurement documents during the procedure.
source: Amicus_2.docx — expert legal memo (section VI), provided 2026-06-02
notes: Both cases present with their gold paragraphs.
